events.title: Auburn Party on the Plaza
events.loc: City Hall Plaza , 25 West Main St., Auburn
events.desc: I have yet to play mini golf this summer, but it’s at the top of my to-do list. This fab fest brings a mini golf course, food trucks and live music to City Hall Plaza. Sounds like a perfect summer evening to me! And grown-ups, take note: There’s a beer and wine garden for the 21-and-older crowd.

events.title: Renton River Days
events.desc: This weekend festival is a 40-year-old tradition, but every summer brings something new. Grab a spot early on Saturday for the parade, let the kids burn off energy bouncing around inflatables, and even catch a circus performance. Staying up past bedtime? Don’t miss the live music and food trucks during River Nights at Legacy Square!

events.title: Bite of Seattle 2026
events.loc: Seattle Center’s Fisher Pavilion , 305 Harrison St., Seattle
events.desc: More than 300 local vendors are setting up shop at Seattle Center this weekend, so you can indulge in everything from pizza and smashburgers to spicy wontons and cake in a can. Watch cooking demos, listen to live music and snap a few photos. Plus, you can stick around Friday night to catch “Wonka” on the big screen.

events.title: Kids Activations at Bellevue Arts Fair Weekend
events.desc: The Bellevue Arts Fair Weekend showcases the work of more than 350 artists, along with live music and cultural performances. That should be reason enough to go, but the kids might need a little more convincing. Enter: interactive art projects, face painting, sidewalk chalk, Lego bricks and more family-friendly programming to keep them smiling. Activations can be found at Bellevue Arts Museum, C…

events.title: Seafair Torchlight Parade
events.loc: Seattle Waterfront along Alaskan Way , Intersection of Alaskan Way and Broad Street, Seattle
events.desc: Seafair is just around the corner, and this epic waterfront parade is here to kick things off! Snag your seats early and wait for floats, marching bands and more surprises to make their way down the street. And if you’re wondering what to do during the day on the waterfront, I have you covered there too.

events.title: Tacoma Heritage Days: Music
events.loc: Old Town Park , 2350 N. 30th St., Tacoma
events.desc: An incomplete list of instruments my 4-year-old daughter has discovered she can play loudly: harmonica, kazoo, ukulele, kalimba, drums and piano. While I’d love a few moments of peace and quiet, I still can’t resist this musical celebration! Make your own instruments, join a musical parade and even experience an instrument petting zoo during this fantastic celebration. There will be crafts and …

events.title: Greenwood Games
events.loc: Along Greenwood Avenue , North 65th Street to North 80th Street, Seattle
events.desc: Game night takes on a new meaning during this neighborhood scavenger hunt. Grab a playbook and get to puzzle-solving as you walk along Greenwood Avenue. Clues will be posted in business windows, and local game designers will be stationed with their games at stops along the way.

events.title: Nubian Jam Community Fest
events.loc: Forest Park , 802 E. Mukilteo Blvd., Everett
events.desc: Celebrate African and African American culture this weekend with live music and entertainment, from gospel and R&B to reggae and rap. Experience African cuisine and fantastic fashion, shop from local vendors, and learn about local and national history. There will also be a basketball tournament, an open mic contest and special ceremonies.

events.title: Seattle Outdoor Theater Festival
events.desc: I have yet to introduce my daughter to live theater, and this free outdoor theater festival is such a fantastic opportunity to do just that! Multiple theater companies bring the festival to life across several stages, with Shakespearean productions ranging from “A Midsummer Night’s Dream” to “Macbeth” and “As You Like It.”

events.title: Pista sa Nayon
events.loc: Seward Park , 5900 Lake Washington Blvd. S., Seattle
events.desc: Pack your picnic blanket and head to one of my favorite parks for this celebration of Filipino culture and traditions. From delicious food to lively music and dance, this is a vibrant festival you won’t want to miss. Kids can expect games and activities, and parents can shop for handmade crafts and more.
